description: Short-form video showcasing a signature food product with sensory-focused captions that highlight visual appeal and taste, using colloquial Arabic and food emojis to create desire and relatability.
structural_recipe: Film a close-up, high-quality video of the hero product (fried chicken, pastry, etc.) in golden/appetizing lighting. Highlight texture, color, and crispness through 3-5 second dynamic shots. Pair with a casual, conversational caption in Saudi Arabic dialect that: (1) acknowledges a common decision pain point or craving, (2) presents the product as the solution, (3) emphasizes sensory qualities (appearance, taste), (4) uses 2-3 food or heart emojis, (5) includes branded hashtag. Post to Feed or Reels.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences respond to food content that feels personal and relatable rather than corporate. The colloquial dialect signals authenticity and peer-to-peer recommendation. Presenting products as solutions to everyday dilemmas (indecision, cravings) leverages Saudi decision-making patterns where social proof and familiar endorsement drive purchase intent. Golden, visually perfect product shots tap into aspirational aesthetic consumption.
transplantation_caveats: Dialect must be authentic Saudi Arabic—non-native or formal Modern Standard Arabic reduces relatability and engagement
Product must appeal to Saudi taste preferences and dietary norms; pork products and alcohol are non-starters
Video quality and lighting are non-negotiable; poor production quality underperforms despite strong caption copy
Success is sector-specific to F&B; transplanting to fashion, beauty, or services requires structural redesign
cultural_constraints: Must respect halal certification and Islamic dietary principles
Avoid imagery or messaging that could conflict with family-oriented values
Female presenters/hands should be framed appropriately; cultural sensitivity around food preparation
